title: HPEXPIRETIME
description: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in msec.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

returns.description: Returns a list which contains for each field in the request: - `-2` if the field does not exist, or if the key does not exist. - `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expire time. - A positive integer representing the expiration Unix timestamp in milliseconds, if the field has an associated expiration time.

params.description: one or more fields to get the TTL for.
params.name: fields

returns.description: a list of Long values for each of the fields provided: expiration time as a UNIX timestamp in milliseconds; -1 indicating the field has no expiry time set; -2 indicating there is no such field @since 6.4

params.description: one or more fields to get the TTL for.
params.name: fields

returns.description: a list of Long values for each of the fields provided: expiration time as a UNIX timestamp in milliseconds; -1 indicating the field has no expiry time set; -2 indicating there is no such field @since 6.4

params.description: one or more fields to get the TTL for.
params.name: fields

returns.description: a list of Long values for each of the fields provided: expiration time as a UNIX timestamp in milliseconds; -1 indicating the field has no expiry time set; -2 indicating there is no such field @since 6.4

content:
`HPEXPIRETIME` has the same semantics as [`HEXPIRETIME`](), but returns the absolute Unix expiration timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch instead of seconds. ## Examples ``` redis> HSET mykey field1 "hello" field2 "world" (integer) 2 redis> HEXPIRE mykey 300 FIELDS 2 field1 field2 1) (integer) 1 2) (integer) 1 redis> HPEXPIRETIME mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2 1) (integer) 1715705913659 2) (integer) 1715705913659 ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="hpexpiretime-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field: - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in milliseconds. -tab-sep- * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field: - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in milliseconds. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
redis> HSET mykey field1 "hello" field2 "world"
(integer) 2
redis> HEXPIRE mykey 300 FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
redis> HPEXPIRETIME mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1715705913659
2) (integer) 1715705913659
intro: `HPEXPIRETIME` has the same semantics as [`HEXPIRETIME`](), but returns the absolute Unix expiration timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch instead of seconds.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `HPEXPIRETIME` has the same semantics as [`HEXPIRETIME`](), but returns the absolute Unix expiration timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch instead of seconds.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
```
redis> HSET mykey field1 "hello" field2 "world"
(integer) 2
redis> HEXPIRE mykey 300 FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
redis> HPEXPIRETIME mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1715705913659
2) (integer) 1715705913659
```

sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="hpexpiretime-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field:
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in milliseconds.

-tab-sep-

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field:
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in milliseconds.

{{< /multitabs >}}
